Sprint 9 replay infrastructure confirmed on disk: (1) backend/core/contracts/replay_manifest_v1.py — ReplayManifestV1 Pydantic model with 30+ version/hash fields (unit_registry_version, ratio_registry_version, cluster_schema_hash, insight_graph_version, confidence_model_version, scoring_policy_hash, evidence_registry_hash, state_transition_hash, causal_layer_hash, calibration_hash, arbitration_hash, explainability_hash, burden_hash, lifestyle_input_hash, schema_hashes dict, analysis_result_version); (2) backend/core/models/results.py:99-102 — AnalysisResult.replay_manifest: Optional[Dict[str,Any]] 'Sprint 9: ReplayManifestV1 for determinism/replay'; (3) backend/core/models/results.py:399-402 — AnalysisDTO.replay_manifest: Optional[Dict[str,Any]] 'Sprint 9: ReplayManifestV1 for determinism/replay (version stamps + schema hashes)'; (4) backend/migrations/versions/add_replay_manifest_column.py — Alembic migration adding replay_manifest JSON column to analysis_results table (Sprint 9, 2025-02-15). Cursor MUST read replay_manifest_v1.py in full before proposing or evaluating which Required metadata fields are missing vs. already captured inside the replay_manifest JSON blob.

backend/core/contracts/replay_manifest_v1.py:21 — ReplayManifestV1 uses ConfigDict(frozen=False, extra='forbid'). All other governed contracts (BiomarkerScore, AnalysisResult, AnalysisDTO, ConsumerDomainScoreV1) use frozen=True. The immutability principle in this sprint (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:16-20) requires that generated result payloads be immutable. frozen=False on ReplayManifestV1 is inconsistent with the immutability principle. Cursor must assess whether this is intentional (mutable during assembly, then serialised as JSON into the immutable DTO) or a contract defect, and document the finding in the audit report.

Comparing automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:155-176 (Required metadata model) against existing models: PRESENT (may be inside replay_manifest JSON): engine component versions (unit_registry_version, ratio_registry_version, etc.), analysis_result_version, lifestyle_input_hash, schema_hashes dict. NOT PRESENT in any current model or contract: result_version_id (distinct from analysis_id), parent_analysis_id / source_analysis_id (lineage link), raw_input_hash, parsed_biomarker_payload_hash, questionnaire_hash, single engine_version / pipeline_version fields, knowledge_estate_version, active_authority_manifest_hash, compiled_estate_index_hash, compile_manifest_hashes (schema_hashes exists but scope differs), supersedes_result_version_id, superseded_by_result_version_id, regeneration_reason, result_payload_hash. Cursor must map each proposed field against the existing replay_manifest_v1.py content before proposing additions or migration.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:184-197 — 12 scope items, all bounded. Items 1-8 are investigation/definition only (no code). Items 9-12 allow bounded implementation: 'minimal metadata/stale detection only if safely bounded' (item 9), 'add tests if implementation occurs' (item 10), 'produce design/audit report' (item 11), 'update launch-readiness audit' (item 12). Implementation boundary (lines 202-215) enumerates allowed bounded examples: classification helper, stale-detection function, API metadata field, frontend warning/banner, classification-only tests. Scope is defined.

backend/core/pipeline/orchestrator.py and backend/core/pipeline/orchestrator_phases_v1.py are Intelligence Core (SOP §3: pipeline logic). If Cursor modifies any file under backend/core/pipeline/, the HIGH risk controls apply — no silent behavioural drift, determinism must be preserved, ARCH-RT guardrails must still pass. The sprint STOP conditions (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:329-339) items 6 and 7 protect this surface: STOP if implementation touches clinical interpretation logic or weakens ARCH-RT guardrails. Cursor must NOT modify orchestrator behaviour as part of stale-result classification — classification logic must live in a new helper, not inside the pipeline itself.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:121-127 — non-negotiable architectural rule: 'Generated result payloads are immutable. Do not silently mutate or overwrite old generated results.' automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:212-215 — explicit prohibitions: 'Do not implement full destructive refresh. Do not implement broad database migration unless hardening confirms it is safe and bounded.' STOP conditions (lines 333-334): 'STOP if: regeneration would overwrite old results / implementation would require broad schema migration.' Closure requirements (lines 375-376) enforce: 'no destructive refresh logic is included.' These protections are comprehensive.

backend/core/contracts/replay_manifest_v1.py:14-174 — ReplayManifestV1 captures 30+ deterministic version/hash fields. Cursor must: (1) read the full contract before the investigation begins; (2) map each Required metadata field (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:155-176) against existing replay_manifest_v1.py fields and produce a gap table in the audit report; (3) not propose new DB columns or contract fields for data that is already captured inside the replay_manifest JSON blob without explaining why a structured top-level field is required instead. Rationale: Sprint 9 already built substantial replay infrastructure; the audit must characterise what exists before proposing additions, per the prompt's own instruction at line 177: 'Do not invent fields blindly. First inspect current persistence model.'

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:320-325 — always run: python backend/scripts/validate_day_one_architecture.py + python -m pytest backend/tests/architecture/test_day_one_architecture_guardrails.py -q. If implementation occurs, also run stale-result classification tests, immutable snapshot policy tests, result version metadata serialisation tests, API stale flag tests if added, frontend stale banner tests if added, no-destructive-overwrite tests (lines 310-317). Regression coverage is defined.

Sprint goal is to EXTEND deterministic replay capability, not change it. Allowed bounded implementation (classification helper, stale flag, UI banner) adds new output fields but does not alter existing analytical computation. STOP condition 5 (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:336): 'STOP if deterministic replay cannot be supported with current metadata' — exits to design-only if implementation would compromise determinism. STOP condition 4 (line 335): 'STOP if regeneration would overwrite old results' — prevents destructive state mutation. Determinism is protected.

changes.result: NO STOP CONDITIONS TRIGGERED IN HARDENING
changes.citation: Hardening review: (1) raw input availability — cannot be determined pre-execution (runtime DB state), correctly deferred to Cursor investigation; (2) persistence model unclear — NOT unclear, backend/core/models/results.py and replay_manifest_v1.py are well-defined; (3) broad schema migration — NOT required at this stage; the replay_manifest JSON column already exists from Sprint 9; (4) destructive overwrite — NOT included, explicitly prohibited; (5) deterministic replay feasibility — Sprint 9 infrastructure exists, replay_manifest_v1.py captures extensive version/hash fields; (6) clinical interpretation — NOT touched by allowed bounded scope; (7) ARCH-RT guardrails — validators will be run; (8) stale-result UI product decision — correctly scoped as a decision to define (option A-D), not as an implementation requirement; (9) test provability — classification logic is testable. No hardening STOP triggered.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:194 — 'Implement minimal metadata/stale detection only if safely bounded.' automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:213-215 — 'Do not implement full regeneration job unless explicitly approved after investigation.' The sprint's STOP condition 3 (line 333): 'STOP if implementation would require broad schema migration.' Sprint 9 already added replay_manifest JSON column — any new metadata fields Cursor proposes must either: (a) be stored inside the existing replay_manifest JSON blob (no migration required), or (b) require a new Alembic migration only if the field cannot be stored inside the blob AND the migration is narrow and safe. Cursor must not implement a new DB migration without declaring it as a bounded change and confirming it does not touch existing column values.

Out-of-scope list (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:289-304) prohibits: overwriting persisted result payloads, deleting historical results, destructive refresh, clinical scoring logic, card evidence logic, root-cause logic, PSI status, SignalRegistry, SignalEvaluator, biomarker SSOT, unit conversion, frontend interpretation logic beyond stale messaging, fallback parsers, broad regeneration jobs without approval. Closure requirements (lines 372-377) enforce: no destructive refresh, no clinical interpretation changes, no ambiguous stash, only in-scope files.
